title: Cameron's World
description: A web-collage of text and images excavated from the buried neighbourhoods of GeoCities.

heading.text: A love letter to the Internet of old

bodyText.text: GeoCities was a web-hosting service that made it possible for people to build their own homepages. During the 90s, users

sample.text: A love letter to the Internet of old
GeoCities was a web-hosting service that made it possible for people to build their own homepages. During the 90s, users
By the time the US service shut down in October 2009, there were over 38 million GeoCities pages. Cameron’s World brings
In an age where we interact primarily with branded and marketed web content, Cameron’s World is a tribute to the lost da

worldSystems.name: Collage Core
worldSystems.reason: tags include net.art; description mentions collage; archetype affinity: collage field

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: archetype affinity: collage field; large display type

archetype.name: Collage Field
archetype.reason: tags include net.art; description mentions collage; title mentions world; large display type

mechanics.interactionModel: The page behaves like a field of scraps, hyperlinks, stickers, and media fragments; discovery comes from scanning and touching layers.
mechanics.navigationModel: Users browse clusters or islands of content rather than a clean linear document.
mechanics.stateModel: Field at rest -> hover or focus cluster -> reveal linked artifact -> continue browsing.
engineHints: Use ordinary DOM layering first; only add Canvas or WebGL for background texture, not for the whole experience.
Use sprite or GIF assets as materials inside a disciplined z-order system.
Use hover swaps, link states, and offset scroll rather than overbuilt component abstractions.
motionRecipe: Use layered reveals, jitter, and hover swaps selectively.
Let movement imply depth between scraps rather than simulating real 3D.
Keep one stable composition anchor so the field does not turn into noise.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Flatten the field into a guided scavenger feed or chapter stack while preserving overlap, stickers, and hyperlink energy.
implementationNotes: Keep one hierarchy anchor such as a headline, map, or rail so the collage stays legible.
Treat every scrap as part of one composition, not a random sticker dump.
Use link states and media swaps as the interaction language.
antiPatterns: Do not let the collage become unreadable on small screens.
Do not animate every layer continuously.
Do not remove the hyperlink density that gives the field its energy.
validationChecklist: One anchor element keeps the field navigable.
Mobile preserves density through chapters instead of raw chaos.

designGuidance.implementationPrompt: Build this as a Collage Core page with a secondary cue from Playable Poster using the Collage Field interaction model. Keep Comic Neue as the voice anchor, preserve the palette around #060507, #5f5c5c, #55291e, drive the page through scroll, hover, tap, and implement the mobile fallback as: Flatten the field into a guided scavenger feed or chapter stack while preserving overlap, stickers, and hyperlink energy.
designGuidance.summary: Collage Core + Collage Field. The page behaves like a field of scraps, hyperlinks, stickers, and media fragments; discovery comes from scanning and touching layers.
